az.title: TypeScript Union Types

content.text: TypeScript-d…ô union types bir d…ôyi≈ü…ônin bir neΟß…ô f…ôrqli tipd…ôn biri ola bil…ôc…ôyini gΟΕst…ôrir. Bu, `|` simvolu il…ô t…ôyin edilir. M…ôs…ôl…ôn, `string | number` d…ôyi≈ü…ônin ya s…ôtir, ya da r…ôq…ôm ola bil…ôc…ôyini bildirir.

Union types `:` simvolu il…ô d…ôyi≈ü…ôn…ô v…ô ya parametr…ô t…ôyin edilir. D…ôyi≈ü…ôn yalnΡ±z t…ôyin edilmi≈ü tipl…ôrd…ôn biri ola bil…ôr. S…ôhv tip istifad…ô ets…ôn, TypeScript x…ôb…ôrdarlΡ±q ver…ôr. A≈üaΡüΡ±da kod nΟΦmun…ôl…ôri il…ô baxaq.

items.description: Bir d…ôyi≈ü…ôn bir neΟß…ô tipd…ôn biri ola bil…ôr.

- **T…ôhlΟΦk…ôl…ôr**: Ο΅ox sayda union type istifad…ôsi kodu mΟΦr…ôkk…ôbl…ô≈üdir…ô bil…ôr. `any` il…ô qarΡ±≈üdΡ±rmaq TypeScript-in faydasΡ±nΡ± azaldΡ±r.

en.title: TypeScript Union Types
en.description: Union Types in TypeScript allow a variable to be one of several types, providing flexibility and type safety.

content.text: Union Types in TypeScript let a variable be one of multiple types, defined using the `|` symbol. For example, `string | number` means a variable can be either a string or a number.
Union Types are assigned using the `:` symbol. A variable can only hold one of the specified types. Using an incorrect type triggers a TypeScript warning. See code examples below.

items.description: A variable can be one of several types.
Parameters or return values can use union types.
Union types can be used for array elements.

content.text:
- **Dangers**: Using too many union types can complicate code. Mixing with `any` reduces TypeScriptβÄôs benefits.
- **Recommendations**: Use minimal union types, narrow types when possible, and avoid `any`.
